text: I arrive as career switcher deciding whether this can replace another course. My past: I have bought online courses before and often quit when the path became vague after the first week.
Today I want to: Understand what I would learn, how long it takes, and whether there is proof it works.
My life goal is: Build a career that gives me independence without wasting money on empty promises.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: self-degree - AI Learning Platform That Replaces Traditional School

text: I see "self-degree - AI Learning Platform That Replaces Traditional School".
I think: I can only see the title, so I am holding back judgment until I know what the learning path actually contains.
My reaction: The promise sounds bold and interesting, but it also triggers my fear of another course that starts strong and turns vague.
Idea: show a clear sample path with time commitment, outcomes, and proof from past learners right away.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month of life on one more look is reasonable if it quickly shows whether this can support my career switch without empty promises.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is cautiously curious with low confidence because I have almost no evidence yet.
